reviews.review_msg: Elspeth was open and respectful of my ideas, and also gave an honest reactions - probably only one of two profs who have ever used feedback to build on my projects rather than drastically change them Also avoid being aggressively pretentious - ie talk a lot but only got a whole lot of nothing

Elspeth's approach to creation in this class was invaluable! She really inspires one to create thoughtfully, with attention to process and exploration, avoiding the preciousness of an ultimate piece. She leaves you to your own devices if she sees you challenging yourself, but is always available. She's an absolute delight. Highly recommended!

Elspeth has definitely changed the way I approach my artwork, her explaination of process is enlightening and she is the nicest person in the world on top of everything. A really great teacher for an otherwise slightly boring course. I would always recommend her to anybody

Great teacher.. I love that everything that she has taught me is what I wanted to learn in the first place.. What it is most important is loving what we do not for its aesthetics but for the true meaning of art. Has made me love art even more! THUMBS UP

I don't know why people complain...she's a wonderful, passionate teacher who assigns projects that are challenging yet really interesting. DO NOT take a class with her if you aren't open to experimenting, and/or take yourself too seriously! she won't tell you much from the start, but this is university, and you're expected to ask questions...

Really nice person but lousy at giving feedback - she always tries to steer you in HER preferred direction according to her own personal biases, rather than really trying to understand what YOU (the student) are trying to accomplish.  A teacher should be able to inspire you to grow in your own way, not confine you to her approach only.

Elspeth is one of the best instuctors at SFU, and deserves far more recognition than what is given to her. Anyone so fortunate to take a course with her should really use her as an asset because she is a wealth of incredible insight and information.

Not AT ALL clear what her expectations are.  She doesn't explain the instructions for activities AT ALL - just hands you a piece of paper with vague directions, and then treats you like an idiot when you ask for clarification.  Doesn't give you any idea of how you are doing in class, and most of the projects are kidnergarten level. Waste of money.

WONDERFUL WOMAN!!!  She's very gentle and calm - her feedback is honest and ranks the highest (out of all the feedback I've experienced from SFU profs) in helpfulness and constructiveness.  GREAT TEACHER!!!
Nice prof, but kind of boring. Some of the projects she comes up with are really weird and I didn't enjoy them at all. Overall, she is nice, but the classes are not that enjoyable.
